characters.name: Salome (Dance of the Seven Veils figures)

event: Costume Design for the Dance of the Seven Veils
symbols: mirror — vanity and self-absorption of Salome in the story of John the Baptist's death
dance — the seductive performance that led to the beheading of John the Baptist
veils and layered drapery — the seven veils of the dance tradition surrounding the biblical narrative
description: An avant-garde costume design showing two stylized female figures in angular, geometric poses. The figures wear elaborate layered costumes in bold reds, greens, oranges, and yellows with sharp angular pleats and folds. One figure holds a circular mirror, the other appears to hold a tambourine or round object. Their faces are rendered in a Cubo-Futurist style with sharp planes and bold outlines. The background is solid black, emphasizing the vivid colors of the costumes.
theological_description: Ekster's costume design for the Dance of the Seven Veils connects to the biblical narrative of Salome's dance before Herod Antipas, which led to the execution of John the Baptist. Mark records: 'When Herodias's daughter came in and danced, she pleased Herod and his guests. And the king said to the girl, Ask me for whatever you wish, and I will give it to you... She went out and said to her mother, For what should I ask? And she said, The head of John the Baptist' (Mark 6:22-24). While the 'Dance of the Seven Veils' is not in the biblical text but derives from later theatrical tradition (notably Oscar Wilde's play), it draws on the scriptural narrative of how Herodias used her daughter's seductive dance to achieve the murder of God's prophet. The mirror in the design may symbolize the vanity and self-regard that drove Herodias's deadly scheme. John had condemned Herod's unlawful marriage to Herodias (Mark 6:18), and this dance was her instrument of revenge against the truth-teller.

scripture_refs.reason: The dance of Herodias's daughter before Herod that led to John the Baptist's beheading — the biblical source
Matthew's parallel account of the dance and the execution of John the Baptist

_meta.title: Costume design for Dance of the Seven Veils
